post_rendered:
Welcome to Mafia Game 176: One Last Chance

If you are not currently alive in this game, please do not post in this thread. Visit this thread to sign up for future games.
Credits to Binomial-theorem/chaotic_iak/willwang123 (among others) for the rule template.

Story
The town of Fendicin was falling apart.

It was in an awkward geographical position to begin with, straddled in the warzone between two nations, and neither truly cared for its survival. They, in fact, were perfectly content with robbing the town when they happened across it. The privileged got away quickly, and the rest were left... to suffer. The roads hadn't been maintained in months, the shops all closed, and everyone was left to fend for themselves.
So it was only a matter of time until some of the townspeople turned on the others. Why wait for our demise, they figured, when we could band together to feed upon the others and escape? They wreaked havoc in the small town, showing no qualms about robbing or even murdering their former neighbors and friends. The rest had already given up hope.

And that's when they appeared.

Nobody saw when it happened; one day the town center was empty, and the next it simply had things, which looked like they couldn't have been put up in less than a week. A small circle of pedestals and a series of buttons on remotes - 8, to be exact - on a simple table in the middle. On the edge of the square was a small cage beside another button.
"What are these buttons for?" One man asked, giving the question they had all been wondering but couldn't bring themselves to ask.
Another replied, "There's only one way to find out."
The entire town, or what was left of it, spent the rest of the day experimenting with the buttons. They discovered the purposes of five of the buttons before night fell.
The townspeople went to sleep, content on their work for the day. But when they awoke on the next, they found something terrible.
The final three buttons were missing.
There was only one possibility - the bandits, greedy for their own power, had stolen them. The villagers had no clue what the buttons did, and could only hope they were not significantly more powerful; if that were the case, they were screwed. They discussed their options, and then realized they had no clue what the last button, the one near the cage, did.
One pressed the cage button, and they watched in amazement as the bottom of the cage fell, leaving a deep hole. This was a button of death, and one by one, the citizens realized that the button could be used as a weapon against the bandits.

The thirteen remaining people in the town looked at each other. The bandits among them were worried, of course, but the others felt ecstasy. A work of a divine being, or whatever it was, they were given a chance.

And they were determined to make use of it.


Rules
  • Read the rules. Including this one.
  • Follow all the rules. Including this one.
  • Do not post at night.
  • You may PM me at any time and ask me anything, such as rule clarification. Questions asked in-thread will only be answered in-PM, if at all, unless I specifically believe it is something worth telling the general public.
  • Do not talk about this game outside of this topic to anyone other than the moderators, unless you are specifically allowed to do so.
  • You may not use any formatting that has an effect of concealing text. This includes size=1, hide="", color=#FFFFFF, and color=white, among others.
  • Do not edit or delete posts you have already submitted; this includes editing right after you post (what goes in the thread stays in the thread). If you for any reason find the need to change your post, use EBWOP or EBWON as necessary.
  • Do not pretend to edit or otherwise pretend to break a rule.
  • If you are dead, do not post anything else about the game anywhere. You may still spectate the game, but you may no longer post in this thread.
  • Do not edit the tags on the thread. This also applies if you are not playing the game.
  • There is no confirm rule in this game. That does not excuse you from knowing the rules.


Voting/Hanging
  • This game is based on deadline hanging. The player with the most votes at the time the deadline is reached is hanged. Ties will be explained in the next section. Starting Day 2, majority hanging is enabled, which means day will end early if at any time a player has more than half the amount of total votes on them. If majority hang is not reached, deadline hanging occurs.
  • If there is a tie vote at the end of the day between two players, the result of it will be randomized. However, if there are an equal number of votes for No Hang and a player, No Hang will be the result.
  • Post votes in bold font in the format "Vote: <insert player here>". For example, "Vote: fdas" is an acceptable vote, while "Vote: fdas", "Vote fdas", "vote: fdas", "Vote:fdas", "Vote: fads", "Vote: Fdas" and "Vote: fdas" are not.
  • Spell the username of the player you wish to vote for exactly. Do not use nicknames.
  • Improperly made votes (including votes in hide tags or any of the unacceptable forms above) will not be counted under any circumstances. I will not tell you if your vote is invalid. There is one exception to this - see below.
  • The characters "I" (capital i) and "l" (lowercase L) are considered the same character for the purpose of voting. That is, attempting to fakevote by substituting one of these characters in will not work. Most other fakevotes are acceptable, but please PM me to confirm that it is. If we do not notice that you fakevoted, it may be counted as a real vote, so it is a good idea to PM me every time you intend to fakevote.
  • You may vote for no hanging to occur during a day. Vote this by including in your post "Vote: No Hang". (Note: This is not the same thing as unvoting.)
  • You do not have to unvote before making a new vote, though it is appreciated if you do so. Unvote in the format "Unvote". An unvote will undo your previous vote, if any.
  • Starting Day 2, if at any point in a day more than half of the players currently alive are voting for a single player, that player will be hanged. It will then immediately be twilight.
  • Starting Day 2, if at any point in a day at least half of the players alive are voting to not hang, then nobody will be hanged. It will then be twilight.
  • Voting for Events is described in the "Special Mechanics" section. Please refer there for any specifics.
  • The moment the deadline passes, no more votes will be counted even if the mod has not made a post.
  • During twilight (the time between the end of deadline and the moderator's "do not post" post), players may still post. However, no more votes and/or unvotes will be processed.
  • Once I post a end-of-day scene, the person hanged (if any) is officially dead. Night then immediately begins. You may not post during night.


Activity
  • Each day will be 264 hours (11 days). The moderator may extend the deadline for the end of the day at his/her discretion. Note that this will be unlikely.
  • The deadline will occur the very second 264 hours has occurred.
  • Each night will be 3 real-life days long.
  • When the night deadline is met, if the moderator does not receive a night action from either a player or his/her partner(s) (if you have them), the player will lose the chance to perform an action during that night.
  • Night will end at some time after the night deadline when the mod makes the post announcing the end of night and start of the next day.
  • Activity rule for this game: While not on V/LA, you are expected to make at least one post between every two activity checks, which will be done roughly every 48 hours. If you miss this, you may be replaced without warning. Do not try to abuse this by saying "activity post" every two days - I will warn or replace you if I feel you are posting purely to bypass this requirement.
  • If a player is going to be V/LA, he/she must post in bold in-thread or PM me. The moderator will not prod the player unless the V/LA is too long. If your V/LA will be longer than about a week, please either try to find a way to participate in the game during that time or ask for a replacement.


Etiquette
  • Do not use inappropriate language.
  • Do not make any personal attacks.
  • Be mature.
  • For a minor rule infraction, newbies can receive up to two warnings, and SE’s/IC's up to one. If a player commits an infraction after he/she has run out of warnings, or commits a modkill level infraction, he/she will be modkilled or force-replaced, depending on the scenario. Please do not make me modkill you.


Moderators
  • I (summitwei) am the Moderator of Game 176.
  • Red-colored text (#FF0000) is used for official votecounts and activity checks by the moderators. It is also used for mafia flavor-related posts.
  • Blue-colored text (#0000FF) is used for the moderator and co-mod's posts other than votecounts and activity checks.
  • Green-colored text (#00FF00) is used for town flavor-related posts.
  • Please do not do things that have the effect of impersonating a moderator. For the most part, though, posting in colors is fine.
  • The moderator has the last say in interpreting the rules.


Setup Tab
This is a semi-closed setup. This means that the majority of the setup is public knowledge but there is some part of it that is not publicly known.

This setup is for 13 players. It has 9 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Jailkeeper, and 3 Mafia Goons. There is also one special mechanic; see the "special mechanic" tab for further details.

Mafia may discuss at any time. Mafia's nightkill is compulsive, meaning that Mafia must kill someone each night. If the Mafia do not select a kill by the deadline, a random player outside of the Mafia faction will be targeted.


Special Mechanics Tab
This setup's special mechanic is Events. An Event can temporary change up the mechanics of the game.

On Day 1, Town will be given two possible Events in the thread. Vote for an event with Select: (name of event), styled as shown. Vote for no event to take place with Select: No Event. All Event votes must happen before 48 hours before the deadline of day.
If an Event receives a majority of votes (over half of currently alive players' votes), it will occur, and whatever effect is stated will happen. Only one Event may take place per day, and No Event cannot reach majority (voting No Event is equivalent to unvoting). If there is not an Event reaches majority by the end of the day, no Event will occur.

Town has a total of five Events; they are ordered randomly, and you will only know them when they are presented. If/When an Event is selected, an unselected event (if there are still any) will take its place, and the next day, Town will once again be presented with two Events, one of which they already knew about.


Mafia is also given three Events that they may use; however, you do not know what these are, or what restrictions are imposed upon them. Several restrictions on Town Events may not be imposed on Mafia Events. The only restriction you do know is that Mafia Events must be chosen 48 hours before the end of a phase.
The occurrence of a Mafia Event will be revealed at the same time the impact of the Event would take place.


Role PM's
Vanilla Townie wrote:
Hello (player),


You are a Vanilla Townie.


Wincondition: You win when all threats to the Town have been eliminated.

Abilities:
You have no special abilities. Your power lies in your voice and your vote.


Good luck!
Town Jailkeeper wrote:
Hello (player),


You are a Town Jailkeeper.


Wincondition: You win when all threats to the Town have been eliminated.

Abilities:
Jailkeep: Every night, you may PM me the name of a player. That player will be jailkept; they will simultaneously be prevented from doing actions, while also being protected from a nightkill.

Good luck!
Mafia Goon wrote:
Hello (player),

You are a Mafia Goon.


Wincondition: You win when there are as many Mafia as there are Town.

Abilities:
Mafia: At any point during the game, you may discuss with your fellow Mafia [here].
Nightkill: Every night, somebody in the Mafia faction may choose someone to be targeted for a nightkill.

The Mafia team consists of:
(list of Mafia players)

Good luck!


Alive (3)
  1. math_turtle37 HannahW
  2. Kbwuf
  3. mathjay


Dead (10)
Made_in_2004 was hanged Day 1 (Vanilla Townie) *
fdas was killed Night 1 (Vanilla Townie) *
Minty_AYZ was killed Day 2 (Mafia Goon) *
sudzoof was killed Day 2 (Vanilla Townie) *
avanders was killed Day 2 (Vanilla Townie) *
tikkitamatoa was hanged Day 2 (Vanilla Townie) *
hdgehrke was killed EOD 2 (Vanilla Townie) *
zdtsd was killed Night 3 (Vanilla Townie) *
benjaminsz was killed Day 4 (Mafia Goon) *
NDMath was killed Night 4 (Vanilla Townie) *


Significant Events
Day 1: The Election Event is selected *
Minty_AYZ replaces noahdavid *
Day 1 ends *
Night 1 ends *
Day 2: BOOM BYE TOWN is activated *
Day 2: Dayvig is used *
Day 2 ends *
Day 2: Vengefulness is used *
HannahW replaces math_turtle37 *
Night 2 ends *
Day 3: BOOM BYE SCUM is selected *
Day 3 ends *
Night 3 ends *
Day 4: BOOM BYE TOWN is used *


Warnings
sudzoof - excessive text hiding *


Do not post until this line is crossed out.
You may post.
The deadline for Day 4 is 1/3 at 9:40PM EST.

The two Town Events for D1 are as follows:

Election: This night, all town will be given the ability to vote for a player for an "election" (players may not vote themselves). The winner of this election will be announced at the start of the next day, and they will be given a 1-Shot Day Vigilante to use that day.
Vengefulness: If a town player is hanged this day, that player may choose to select another player, who will be immediately killed.

Vote for them with Select: Election and Select: Vengefulness respectively.

post_rendered: The problem with Venge is that it also gives the power if town is hung, and if that town chooses to Venge another town, we lose 3 town in one day. Additionally, there is no discussion about who gets venged. The hung town player acts independently. Dead people, especially D1, don’t often have the best ideas who mafia is, and I can see this going down very badly. That being said, election is a little more risky, as it gives a possible mafia free kill, but in the case that mafia wins the election, there would still be town discussion. If the mafia vig were to shoot a towny player, or not listen to the sway of the general populous, they would be under suspicion. I think that in general, popular opinion is more reliable than one opinion, unless a really really good town player is hung which would be stupid anyways. So election probably has a better opportunity for outcome.

post_rendered:
Wait choices don't go away? In that case it would be better to keep vengeful for day 2 since it has better impact there.

One thing I would like to add is that vengeful promotes better play. People would be more likely to stop and think, what if they guy I am pushing is actually town and I get him hanged and he kills me. It will help stop tunneling. Furthermore, it forces you to make your arguments actually good. If you get someone hanged over a BS reason they are much much much more likely to come after you because they feel wronged. Also, people would only push hard if they truly believe that the person is scum as if they are scum then they cant use vengeful. So, mafia will be wary of trying to lead hangs.

post_rendered:
Hi yeet no confirm

As someone who has played in this game for a while without improving, I've seen a lot of games in which town attack each other, leading to a town hang. Then the principle actor in that hang is hanged the next day for it and is also town. This is by far the easiest way for town to lose. So I will Vote: Election. After reading all of the arguments for venge, I still am not convinced, because in my experience, by FAR the most likely outcome of venge is town hang, town venge, town nightkill and with that we're at 7v3 already, with a whole 30% that the jk is dead already! That simply isn't acceptable to me. It's difficult for maf to use the 1-shot vig w/o outing themselves, and the odds of town being responsible for a town hang is much higher than the odds of there being a town so scummy that maf can vig them w/o outing themselves. Besides, we have to consider the odds. In venge, there's a 75% chance of town being shot in a vacuum. In election, there's only a 25% chance of maf getting the gun. So there has to be a higher than 2/3 chance of town mis-shooting d2 in order for election to be worse. I don't think we're all that bad at scumhunting. In addition, it's important to remember that math doesn't work in mafia. When you account for the fact that the shots are not being made at random, things swing even further towards the maf. If they know that vengeful is in effect, they will intentionally avoid leading hangs in order to increase the odds of town being venged even further. In addition, under the assumption of a d1 mis-hang, shooting town d2 is not as bad as venging town d1, bc venging town d1 means that in the first 2 days, 5 people die and at least 4 of them are town (2 nk, d1 hang, venge) and shooting town d2 means that 5 people die in the first two days, at least 4 of whom are town (2 nk, 1 shoot, d1 hang). How is this any different, you ask? Because in the missed dayshot, we gain far more info from the flip and have a higher chance to hang scum d2, therefore increasing the odds of the first 5 deaths containing at least one maf. It should also be mentioned that election is still useful if we hang maf d1, therefore increasing town momentum and steamrollering scum (imagine the situation in which we hang scum d1, kill scum d2; after the first two days, there are at least 2 scum dead already, and we could even end the game with the d2 hang).

Wow that turned into a wall quickly. Anyway, vote election yo.

I have come to the conclusion that election can be made identical to town having a second hang on day 2.

It does not matter who we elect, The elected representative is FORBIDDEN from using their kill without the consent of a majority of the town. If they go rogue we hang them.

So, during the day we can have a second, parallel set of votes and when a person receives a majority of the votes, they will get killed by the dayvig. In essence, town has two hangs they can use.

As long as it is not lylo, this will work.

Flavor
The first day was eventful, to say the least. Everybody had some sort of feeling about the cage of death. Excitement, hope, determination... worry, anger, fright.
There was no time to lose. The townspeople began discussing the seemingly simple task of eliminating the 13 down to 3, but of course, nothing is ever truly simple. The day abounded with accusations and defenses, claims and cases, with no true end in sight.

But one stood out.
He was the angry one. Constantly yelling about how he was a good guy and others were not, presenting conspiracy theories that really "everybody else in this darned town is just a murderer in waiting". The others were fed up with him, and some thought this would be their only chance to shut him up, once and for all.

He protested on his way into the cage. "You're all screwed!" He said. "I was your savior, and you let us down! You all caused this!"
"Oh, shut your mouth," the proclaimed executioner said, as she pressed the button.


His screams could be heard for another ten minutes.


In the meantime, they selected a button to press, its outcome to change their situation for the better or for the worse. Whatever happened from there would be up to fate... and really, isn't that better than it being up to those looking to bring you down?

The two Town Events for D3 are as follows:

Double Hang: For this day only, the two players with the most amount of votes will both be hanged. Ties are randed, etc. Note: If No Hang is the top vote, then a player with the most votes (the second highest) will not be hanged.
Null Votes: For this day only, majority is turned off (if it was on before), and Mafia's votes are not counted for the final hang. (For this day, if there is a tie, it will not be stated to be randed, and votecounts will still be sorted by number of voters, regardless of their alignment.)

Vote for them with Select: Double Hang or Select: Null Votes respectively.

Roles and Actions
1. benjaminsz - Mafia Goon
2. hdgehrke - Vanilla Townie
3. math_turtle37 HannahW - Town Jailkeeper
4. Kbwuf - Mafia Goon
5. NDMath - Vanilla Townie
6. zdtsd - Vanilla Townie
7. tikkitamatoa - Vanilla Townie
8. Made_in_2004 - Vanilla Townie
9. sudzoof - Vanilla Townie
10. avanders - Vanilla Townie
11. noahdavid Minty_AYZ - Mafia Goon
12. mathjay - Vanilla Townie
13. fdas - Vanilla Townie

Night 1:
Kbwuf kills fdas
math_turtle37 jailkeeps mathjay
Election Votes:
mathjay votes zdtsd
NDMath votes Minty_AYZ
math_turtle37 votes sudzoof
zdtsd votes benjaminsz
sudzoof votes benjaminsz
hdgehrke votes Kbwuf
tikkitamatoa votes fdas
avanders votes sudzoof
fdas votes benjaminsz

Night 2:
Kbwuf kills zdtsd
HannahW jailkeeps zdtsd

Night 3:
benjaminsz kills zdtsd


All Events
(Taken from my design thread, because I was too lazy to pull them up; they may have been reworded slightly when posting)

Town:
Double Hang: For this day only, the two players with the most amount of votes will both be hanged. Ties are randed, etc. Note: If No Hang is the top vote, then a player with the most votes (the second highest) will not be hanged.
BOOM BYE SCUM: If a Mafia is hanged this day (and the game does not end then), the Mafia will lose their next night kill.
Vengefulness: If a town player is hanged this day, that player may select another player who will be immediately killed.
Null Votes: For this day only, majority is turned off (if it was on before), and Mafia's votes are not counted for the final hang. (For this day, if there is a tie, it will not be stated to be randed, and votecounts will still be sorted by number of voters.)
Election: This night, all town will be given the ability to vote for a player for an "election". The winner of this election will be announced at the start of the next day, and they will be given a 1-Shot Day Vigilante to use that day.

Mafia:
Negate Event: Use after an Event has been selected (but before its mechanic has come into effect). The effect will not happen.
Janitor: Use during day. The hang this day will be janitored; that is, the player who is hanged today will not flip.
BOOM BYE TOWN: Use at any time. Select one Town and one Mafia who are currently alive. Both players will be killed. This may be used multiple times without restriction, but there is a one-phase cooldown before using it again.


General Comments
You'll notice the Mafia Event of Negate Event - this was something scum could have used to seal the deal at LyLo, but had apparently forgotten. So close, yet so far...

Anyways, I think the game ran quite well; play quality was not too bad. Scum played pretty well IMO but kind of lost nerves during LyLo and because of this missed the out. Town had some players who did the bulk of the work (you know who you are, probably) but that's okay.

In terms of the setup - I really liked how the setup ended up running. Game was quite close, and while this isn't necessarily indicative of a balanced setup, I feel the setup shouldn't be too far off-balance. But I do think there was a bit too much kill power in the Town Events. This is something I would reconsider if I were to run this setup again, with different Events.


Probably more stuff worth mentioning but I'll leave this here for now.

post_rendered:
So this is the part where I explain myself

I honestly believed using nullify event would have outed me when I was in the straight shootout with ND. I forgot that town don't know the details of how maf events work.

When I boomed ND I was thinking that if I flipped maf he was conftown, which of course was dumb on my part

And in conclusion, I have decided that, even given the presence of nullify event, this games town events were OP and town won because of it. Everything that sud and ND did was mechanicaly correct. If we'd been smart, we would have nullified every day except the venge day and played like a normal maf game. I should point out that, largely due to events, I went from one of the towniest players in the game to in everyone's POE, before null votes was even a thing.

And here's the part where I argue that I had a good game. Before about halfway through D3, I was one of the most town read players in the game, despite voting for multiple town hangs in one day and even pulling the trigger on one myself. Let's also not ignore the fact that we made it to MyLo in just 2 days and nights, including 5 town kills in one day-night, so that was pretty good.
